finding: A perceived constraint (scarcity) undermined decision quality among affected individuals; interventions reducing perception of the constraint improved subsequent decisions.
A contextual reference point (norm) in the mid-zone of a scale mediated the interpretation of relative terms; this reference point was both category-dependent and accessible in real-time processing.

Individuals facing resource scarcity exhibited increased mental intrusions of cost-related thoughts that were difficult to suppress and changed mental associations, compared to those without such scarcity.

Neural oscillatory patterns (theta in one region, gamma in another) differentially tracked two contrasting psychological states; the presence of one neural signature predicted individual differences in behavioral suppression.
A conjunctive group context boosted individual effort relative to solo performance; however, verbal encouragement from a higher-performing partner attenuated these gains.
A background visual property (simplicity) enhanced attitudes toward an advertisement, the promoted product, and the website; effects were stronger in exposure contexts limiting re-exposure.

Exposure to a diversity-engaging environment improved learning outcomes independent of an individual's own engagement level; spillover effects of a campus's collective engagement benefited all students.

Attending higher-ability educational settings undermined academic self-concept; this effect remained robust and generalizable across numerous individual characteristics, though moderated slightly in some domains.

Teachers' perceptual inaccuracies about students' abilities were more explained by classroom context (socioeconomic and achievement level) than by teacher characteristics; lower-SES and lower-achieving contexts produced systematic underestimation.

Language-minority learners with initial fluency converged to national norms within one year; those with limited initial proficiency remained below average but converged to peer norms (controlling for SES) by middle school.
Individuals with a chronic illness condition exhibited elevated frequencies of genetic variants associated with immune, hormonal, and metabolic pathways compared to population reference data.
An implicit activation procedure influenced moral judgment severity; this effect appeared only when attention to the priming material was low and disappeared when cognitive effort was high.

A training intervention designed to enhance situational awareness improved performance speed and accuracy on critical decision tasks; both intervention and control groups showed high baseline competence.

A flanker-like interference effect (task-irrelevant information affecting task-relevant processing) emerged in a bimodal condition but required shared semantic content rather than response mapping to emerge.
Domain-agnostic statement of conceptual contribution rather than empirical finding; addresses the fundamental role of similarity categorization in cognition and perception.

Single, childless individuals reported lower work absorption than those with other family structures; anticipated domestic responsibilities after work (a proxy for cognitive continuity with work) mediated this relationship.
Domain-agnostic philosophical statement of conceptual purpose rather than empirical finding; addresses the role of norm understanding in shaping causal judgment.
Domain-agnostic philosophical statement on evaluation as foundational to human cognition; does not present empirical findings in abstract.
